content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento. CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Scott S. Lardizabal 
Learning & Development Consultant 
State Compensation Insurance Fund 
900 Corporate Center Drive 
Monterey Park, CA 91754 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-11-171 
Dear Mr. Lardizabal: 
October 6, 2011 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding several provisions of the 
Political Reform Act (the "Act"). 1 Specifically, you requested that we review your state ethics 
training program. We have reviewed your training and with the correction of the few sQJ.all edits 
we discussed over the phone last week, we approve of the portions of the training that cover 
provisions qf the Act. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
isKB:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Zackery P. Morazzini 
General Counsel 
By: 
Sukhi K. Brar 
Counsel, Legal Division 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

sections.question_synthetic: Does the State Compensation Insurance Fund's state ethics training program comply with the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC approves of the portions of the training program that cover provisions of the Political Reform Act, subject to minor edits discussed by telephone.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present; content extracted from body text. Minor typographical error in source ('sQJ.all' for 'small'). Phone discussion referenced but details not provided in document.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does the State Compensation Insurance Fund's state ethics training program comply with the Political Reform Act?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC approves of the portions of the training program that cover provisions of the Political Reform Act, subject to minor edits discussed by telephone.
